sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 10.69 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.10)

statement.latex: Suppose that$S$is a closed oriented surface of genus$g > 1$and$G= _1S=G_1*_A G_2$, where$G_1 A G_2$and the subgroup$A$is finitely generated (and hence free). Is this decomposition geometrical, that is, do there exist connected surfaces$S_1, S_2, T$such that$S=S_1 S_2$,$S_1 S_2=T$,$G_i= _1S_i$,$A= _1T$and embeddings$S_i S$,$T S$induce, in a natural way, embeddings$G_i G$,$A G$?
